title: Clean Fuels Weekly Commentary: CA LCFS Market Sees Modest Price Changes with Increased Type 1 Trade Volumes
content:
This Weekâ€™s Highlights:

(13th January â€“ 19th January)
  • A total of 581,748 credits were exchanged as Type 1 trades, a rise of 30.90% week-over-week (WoW), and all non-zero trades decreased by 7.20% WoW as 1,869,921 credits were traded in CA LCFS.
  • The weekly average CA LCFS credit price increased this week to $73.98/MTÂ for Type 1Â transfers, recording a 0.12%Â rise WoW; All Non-Zero transfers rose by 2.40% in price, reaching $71.54/MT.
  • The average LCFS spot credit price decreased by 1.01% WoW; On 21st Jan, the spot price stood at $69.00/MT. The benchmark price stood at $72.75/MT on 21st
  • D3 RIN prices decreased by 4.38% WoW and D4, D5 and D6 RIN prices increased by 11.11% WoW.

cCarbonâ€™s Market View

LCFS credit prices showed mixed movements, with Type 1 trades gaining modestly while overall non-zero credit prices also strengthened. Trading activity saw a substantial increase in Type 1 volumes, though total non-zero trade volumes declined. Spot credit prices dipped slightly, reflecting minor downfall in daily trading. Feedstock prices have continued to rise as preliminary guidance under Section 45Z has temporarily limited imported used cooking oil (UCO) eligibility for non-sustainable aviation fuel (SAF) pathways. The market for fats, oils, and greases (FOGs) has shown strong momentum, fueled by a notice from the Treasury Department regarding the 45Z Clean Fuel Production Tax Credit guidance, which indicates a potential decrease in demand for imported UCO.

CA LCFS Weekly Price and Volume Trend

[caption id="attachment_591468" align="alignnone" width="1000"] Fig 1. Weekly average credit prices and weekly volumes, Source: California ARB[/caption] The average Type 1 prices recorded a 0.12% WoW rise, increasing from $73.89 to $73.98 this week, with all non-zero credit prices increased by 2.40% WoW, rising from $69.86 to $71.54. Type 1 trades saw 581,748 credits exchanged, reflecting a 30.90% WoW increase. The total transaction volume for all non-zero trades decreased by 7.20% WoW to reach 1,869,921 credits.

CA LCFS Daily Average Spot Price

[caption id="attachment_591480" align="alignnone" width="1000"] Fig 2. California LCFS Weekly average spot prices and benchmark (*CC.Info OTC: Numbers are an average of broker prices reported to CC.info)[/caption] Last week, the average LCFS spot credit prices decreased by 1.01% WoW, reaching $73.55/MT. On 21st January, the average spot price was $69.00/MT, while the benchmark, representing the future price of contracts expiring at the end of 2025, stood at $72.75/MT on 21st January.

CA LCFS Weekly Trade Frequency and Volume

[caption id="attachment_591471" align="alignnone" width="1000"] Fig 3. Trade frequency and volume, Source: California ARB[/caption] As per the data reported by CARB last week, 121 trades were reported in the market, with 20 high-volume trades. The highest transfer by price was $80.00 for a Type 2 transaction at a volume of 20,000 credits. Last week, the lowest transfer by price was $46.50 per credit of Type 2, reported for a transaction at volume of 5,000 credits. The smallest volume transfers of 3 credits of the Type 2 category were undertaken for a price of $76.00, whereas the largest transfer volume was reported at 153,000 MT of the Type 2 category at $72.08.

CA LCFS Monthly Volumes and Price Trend

[caption id="attachment_591472" align="alignnone" width="1000"] Fig 4. Total monthly volumes and associated prices, Source: California ARB[/caption] In December 2024, trading volumes spiked to 7.38M, a notable jump from 2.01M in November, fueled by year-end balance clearing. Average Type 1 prices in December hovered around $71.89, showing a modest decline from November. However, trading activity through 17th January 2025 recorded approximately 4.72M in volume, with Type 1 prices rising to an average of $73.78, signalling sustained market momentum.

CA LCFS Weekly Volume Price Trend

[caption id="attachment_591473" align="alignnone" width="1000"] Fig 5. Volume Price Trend indicator for the CA LCFS market, Source: California ARB[/caption] The Volume Price Trend Indicator (VPT) helps identify the parity between supply and demand for a stock. The idea behind the indicator is to multiply the market volume of LCFS credits by the percentage change in its price. If the credit price declines, the value of the indicator falls. Across 13th Jan â€“ 19th Jan, total VPT recorded a 10.85% increase (WoW), with all non-zero prices rising over the previous week.

Renewable Fuel Standard (RFS): RIN weekly price trend

RINs are credits used for compliance and are the â€œcurrencyâ€ of the RFS program. Renewable fuel producers generate RINs, market participants trade RINs, and obligated parties obtain and then ultimately retire RINs for the compliance list. D3 RIN prices decreased by 4.38% WoW and D4, D5 and D6 RIN prices increased by 11.11% WoW. D3: Cellulosic biofuel D4: Biomass-based diesel D5: Advanced biofuels (produced from any type of renewable biomass except corn starch ethanol) D6: Conventional renewable biofuel ethanol derived from corn starch, or any other qualifying renewable fue

Upcoming dates

Key Clean Fuels Market Developments this week

  • Technical Guidelines for Climate-Smart Biofuel Feedstocks. On January 15, 2025, the U.S. Department of Agriculture (USDA) announced the release of an interim rule on Technical Guidelines for Climate-Smart Agriculture Crops Used as Biofuel Feedstocks. The interim rule establishes guidelines for quantifying, reporting, and verifying the greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions associated with the production of biofuel feedstock commodity crops grown in the United States. These guidelines may inform the subsequent development of requirements for other programs which incentivize low-carbon biofuels.
  • S. Department of Energy Releases 45ZCF-GREET. The U.S. Department of Energy released the 45ZCF-GREET model. This announcement follows the U.S. Department of the Treasury and Internal Revenue Service guidance on the Clean Fuels Production Credit (section 45Z) released on January 10, 2025.
